Ad Text:
Primary Function:

The Financial Reporting Manager is responsible for overseeing the preparation, accuracy, and timely delivery of the organisation’s financial reporting. This role ensures compliance with relevant accounting standards, regulatory requirements, and internal policies. The position plays a key role in financial governance, financial analysis, audit coordination, and continuous improvement of reporting processes.


Responsibilities include:

 Financial Reporting

Lead the preparation of monthly, quarterly, and annual financial reports.

Ensure compliance with applicable accounting standards (e.g., IFRS / AASB / GAAP as applicable).

Prepare statutory financial statements and disclosures.

Maintain accuracy and integrity of financial data and reporting systems.

Provide clear financial insights to senior management to support decision-making.

 

Regulatory and Compliance

Ensure compliance with financial regulations and reporting requirements.

Maintain up-to-date knowledge of accounting standards and regulatory changes.

Manage statutory reporting obligations including tax, FBT, regulatory filings, and corporate reporting requirements.

Develop and maintain financial reporting policies and procedures.

 

Audit Controls

Coordinate external and internal audit processes.

Prepare audit schedules and supporting documentation.

Ensure strong internal financial controls and compliance with internal policies.

Identify and mitigate financial reporting risks.

 

Financial Analysis and Insights

Analyse financial results and provide commentary on variances.

Support budgeting, forecasting, and financial planning processes.

Develop dashboards and reporting tools for management reporting.

Provide strategic insights to senior leadership based on financial data.

 

Team Leadership

Manage and mentor financial reporting staff.

Allocate tasks and oversee workflow within the reporting team.

Support staff development and performance management.

Foster a culture of accuracy, accountability, and continuous improvement.

 

Process Improvement

Identify opportunities to improve financial reporting efficiency and automation.

Implement best practices in financial reporting and controls.

Participate in finance transformation or ERP implementation projects where relevant.

Our Client
Our client is a well established research and marketing business that is at the forefront of marketing science & analytics based advisory.  They work with some of the most well-known brands and CEOs globally supporting their clients make data-led, research driven decisions that ultimately support their growth challenges.

The organisation has had strong growth over the journey diversifying their offering across different markets and verticals to drive sustainable growth. They have done this by constantly developing cutting edge models & constructs which in turn deliver predictive capability of future consumer behaviours.

The Role
This is an executive level leadership position with a focus on driving market & commercial growth across the broader financial services sector, requiring someone to be hands on with clients whilst delivering inspirational leadership to the broader team. There is a clear path for career progression for the right individual into the CEO position.

This role is about establishing and maintaining strong executive relationships with key clients whilst managing the external reputation and brand of the company through active involvement in the business community and industry groups where relevant.

This role is responsible for the financial performance of the division, a substantial & critical component of the role will ensure accurate pipeline management, forecasting and commercial activity to deliver upon business expectations.

The Individual
This role requires an individual that comes from the research & advisory industry, however, also has strong exposure to a specific customer base being financial services to drive deep meaningful commercial relationships. They will be a 'hunter' by nature with a sound track record of driving business growth within both existing customers but also bringing on new logos. They will also;


Be a proven commercial leader that can inspire and drive a high-performance culture. As the team is highly competent with strong experience across analytics & insights it is critical that someone can come into this role and engage the team by building trust & credibility quickly bringing them on the journey to deliver success.
Have executive presence, they will be able to hold themselves competently and confidently across the executive suite, building credibility to deliver compelling outcomes for their clients.
Have a well-rounded background and career, with experience working in a more structured, process driven environment, paired with exposure to smaller more entrepreneurial organisation where they have developed or nurtured a natural growth mindset and want to continue expanding on this.
Be a proven leader, has experience leading high performing teams and has a natural skillset to take their team on a journey to deliver these results.
Be excited about the proposition of career progression, with a plan to take a step up into a CEO position in the near future

We encourage applications from people of all backgrounds as we strive to create an inclusive and diverse working environment. Click on the APPLY button or contact Emma Heffernan at Six Degrees Executive on 0483 946 292 for a confidential discussion.

Six Degrees Executive acknowledges the Traditional Custodians of the lands on which we work and live. We are also committed to equal opportunity and strive to promote diversity, inclusion, belonging, flexibility, and accessibility in all that we do.

Should you require assistance in your interactions with us or through a recruitment process, please let us know so we can provide adjustments for you.

About the Organisation

This organisation is part of a large and highly successful Group with a strong footprint across Australia and New Zealand.

The business continues to invest heavily in its brands, systems and people, and is currently experiencing a period of strong growth and transformation. As the division expands, the finance function is playing an increasingly important role in supporting commercial decision-making and helping leadership navigate this next phase.

About the Role
This is a 6 to 9 month contract role with the potential for extension depending on business needs. 

Reporting directly to the Divisional CFO, you will sit at the heart of the commercial finance team, partnering closely with senior stakeholders across sales, merchandising, operations and marketing. The role is focused on turning financial information into meaningful insight that supports better business decisions.

Day-to-day, you’ll be involved in budgeting and forecasting cycles, analysing business performance across products and channels, and helping leadership understand the key drivers behind revenue, margin and cost movements. You’ll contribute to management reporting, develop dashboards and help improve the quality of financial insight across the division.

There will also be exposure to broader commercial initiatives — including pricing analysis, promotional activity, new product performance and strategic projects that support the division’s continued growth.

What Experience You’ll Need
You will likely come from a commercial finance, FP&A or business partnering background, with experience supporting operational or commercial stakeholders.

Candidates who have worked in retail, consumer goods, apparel or a product-driven environment will naturally find the context familiar, although this isn’t essential.

Strong Excel and financial modelling skills are important, and experience with tools such as Power BI or similar data visualisation platforms would be advantageous. Just as important is the ability to communicate financial insights clearly and work closely with non-finance stakeholders.

You will ideally be CA or CPA qualified, with a solid understanding of financial reporting, performance analysis and commercial decision support.

What’s In It For You
This is a great opportunity to step into a high-visibility commercial finance role, working directly with senior leadership in a business that continues to grow and evolve.

The organisation offers a flexible hybrid working model, a supportive team culture and modern office facilities with on-site parking. The role provides exposure to a highly regarded brand in the retail sector, along with the opportunity to make a genuine commercial impact during a key phase of the business.

ABOUT THE ROLE…

As a Data Architect, you will be responsible for overseeing the development and technical implementation of SG Fleet’s overall data platform strategy aligned with business objectives. The strategy includes data model designs, database development standards, implementation and management of data warehouses, data lake and data analytics systems. This is a full time role and is based at Pymble in Sydney.

TASKS AND RESPONSIBILITIES…

• Responsible for providing expertise in designing, creating, deploying and managing SG Fleet’s data architecture
• Define how data should be stored, consumed, integrated and managed by different data entities and IT systems, as well as any applications using or processing that data
• Express strategic data requirements, outline high level integrated designs to meet these requirements, and align with organisation strategy and related business architecture
• Establish and continuously improve efficient operating models to support a global, federated, domain-oriented data and analytics organisation
• Establish and continuously improve guidelines and guardrails to support governance of quality, non-functional requirements, security and adherence to architectural standards
• Define and manage the flow of data and dissemination of information within the organization
• Collaborate with platform and solution delivery architects to ensure the data analytics platform architecture serves the on-going and strategic business objectives of multiple lines of businesses
• Provide guidance and continuously review the data and analytics solution designs across delivery teams to ensure consistent implementations of architecture standards and scalability of technical solutions

SKILLS AND EXPERIENCE...


• Proven experience in data/application architecture, Cloud Solutions with emerging technologies, Big Data, Data Modeling, Data Architecture, Data Warehousing and Business Intelligence
• Extensive experience in Data modelling (Relational, Dimensional, Star Schema, OLAP Cubes, and Bus Matrix Mapping)
• Demonstrate an advance ability to manage large data sets, cloud computing, open-source tools, and optimization techniques
• A solid background in data and analytics experience implementing and/or overseeing enterprise data and analytics initiatives in enterprise environments
• Previous hands-on experience working with a broad set of Azure services related to Data and Analytics including Azure Data Factory, Azure Data Bricks, Delta Lake, Azure Synapse Analytics
• Understanding of modern data architecture and experience with core data services like maintaining data lakes, data warehouses, data marts as well as relational and dimensional modelling, data quality and master
data management
• Solid experience with Power BI, SSRS and T-SQL and a proven experience of creating reporting or analysis solutions using large volumes of data
• Excellent communication, both written and verbal, along with effective influencing and negotiation skills.
• Experience working in complex projects using Agile methodologies
• Experience and knowledge working within ISO2700X security frameworks

Desirable...

• Broad business experience in complex enterprise environments, ideally within financial services and/or logistics
• Experience with Atlassian suite (Jira, Confluence)

WHAT'S NEXT...

We'd love to hear from you if you're ready to take on your next challenge at a company that embodies diversity and belonging and offer work-life balance and career development.

The Company

This highly regarded industry leader have been at the forefront of online and mobile based product development for many years. A lot of their success has been driven by their increasing strength at using data throughout the business to deliver a high quality products/solutions. 

The Role

A solid BA is required to be one of the interfaces between stakeholders across both business and technology, including full stack development teams. The focus to analyse and define requirements from across a key software engineering team

Elicit and analyse requirements from using a variety of techniques.
Maintain a high level of requirements quality in line with Industry Standards
Collaborate with engineers and subject matter experts to determine the most appropriate technical solution and provide analysis on trade-offs between usability, functionality and performance needs.
Experience Required

Experience facilitating requirements and inception workshops.
A strong understanding of Agile principles and practices (e.g. SCRUM, Lean, Kanban, Extreme Programming, Lean Startup)
Track-record in gaining the confidence of business stakeholders and software engineering building productive working relationships with  technical team members.
A passion for assisting team members by sharing knowledge and/or coaching in analysis and Agile practices. 
Iteration Management experience.
Experience in Unified Modelling Language (i.e. Use Cases and Activity Diagrams).
This is an initial 6 month contract with a view to extend. This is a great opportunity to work across some leading and unique projects. Please reach out to Ross Dunham rdunham@attributegroup.com.au for more information or apply immediately.

Your New Role

The Senior Capability Consultant is responsible for the development and execution of learning initiatives that support an uplift in the priority capabilities of colleagues at the Fund. This role will scope, design, develop and implement learning solutions to meet business objectives and align with key priorities.

This role will partner with Capability Leads on uplifting priority skills across the Fund. This includes a primary focus on working with the Capability Lead, Technology & Data.

Key responsibilities include;


Implement development initiatives across the Fund to build AI, data and technical capability.
Design and implement highly effective, results driven colleague development.
Analysis, design, development, implementation, and evaluation of capability development programs delivered online, face to face, virtual or blended.
Facilitation of workshops and learning activities such as learning circles and communities of practice to support continuous learning across the Fund.
Design and develop continuous learning pathways and performance support tools.
Curate and market learning initiatives using a range of resources including LinkedIn Learning and internally developed content.
Build and develop effective relationships with all levels of the Fund including key stakeholders, people leaders, subject matter experts and colleagues.
Ensure learning needs are accurately identified, and recommendations are appropriate to the learning need using effective development principles.
Develop detailed communication and change management plans to support high impact learning and development solutions.

Due to the Fund's international presence, there can be a requirement to work outside of normal business hours.



What You'll Need

Experience in technology and/or data capability development, applying adult learning principles at scale.
Excellent facilitation skills that engage audiences and enhance the transfer of learning.
Experience creating learning programs from analysis to evaluation.
Proven skills in instructional design for digital, blended and face to face delivery.
Strong project management skills including scoping, and identification of key milestones and deliverables.
Well-developed written and oral communication/ interpersonal skills with the ability to present ideas, perspectives, and issues to senior management.
Extensive experience in working under pressure, setting and managing conflicting priorities and meeting specified timeframes.
Understanding of instructional design methodologies such as ADDIE.
Experience in the use of an LMS to monitor and manage learning content and records such as Oracle.
Experience with design software such as RISE and Storyline 360 (Articulate).

Corporate H/O for an acquisitive Retail & Distribution organisation

FP&A and commercial role partnering State Teams and Group ELT

Based in Liverpool, NSW - $160k- $170k + Super + Car

This is a rare opportunity to join a locally listed business in a highly visible Group role with engagement and influence across the whole business.

Our client is a successful and growing entity that has a proud 50+ year heritage of supplying the local market with luxury products across the Eastern seaboard through a network of national retail brands. This highly visible, commercially focused role will report into the Group Financial Controller and partner the Group CFO and State Business Unit Finance & leadership teams with meaningful management reports, planning, forecasting and strategic decision making.

This role plays a key part in driving financial insight, rigour and forward-looking analysis across the organisation. You will lead group-wide planning and forecasting activities while providing high-quality commercial analysis to support both day-to-day decision-making and longer-term growth initiatives.

Key responsibilities include:

Leading group forecasting, planning and re-forecasting cycles

Ownership of the annual budgeting process across the Group

Delivering detailed financial analysis to support business decisions

Building financial models, scenarios and KPIs to generate actionable insights

Supporting M&A activity, to include modelling, due diligence and integration

Experience requirements:

This role is best suited to an established FP&A manager with strong commercial acumen and a proven track record operating in complex, fast-paced environments.

Strong experience in a staff management and leading through influence

Exceptional financial modelling capability (valuation, scenario and business case analysis)

Advanced Excel and PowerPoint skills; Power BI experience highly regarded

CA or CPA qualified from the Profession (Big 4/ Top Tier Firm) with 2+ years experience within the commercial sector

A proactive, detail-oriented and commercially minded approach

To apply please email your resume to syed.ali@lawsonelliott.com.au quoting job reference number SA17358 or call on 02 8274 0205 for a confidential discussion.

We are more than developers or builders - we are visionaries, and our ability to see the world differently drives us to be bold, embrace innovation and diversity, and lead with optimism. As creators of positive change, we see each new project as an opportunity to leave a lasting legacy, delivering enduring value for all Australians.

We focus on sustainability, innovation, and shared value, to return benefits to our customers, partners, investors, and communities. Join us as we work towards building the imagine nation.

Benefits of working at Mirvac


Permanent position within a supportive and dynamic workplace culture
State-of-the-art head office conveniently located near Circular Quay
Receive $1,000 worth of Mirvac shares
Access to our industry‑leading Learning & Development program to support your career growth

Your Opportunity

We have a fantastic opportunity for a Senior Analyst, FP&A - Development Finance to join our high‑performing team in Sydney. This role supports Development FP&A by delivering timely, accurate analysis and reporting for the Development portfolio.

You will consolidate financial and operational results, lead business improvement initiatives, and build financial models end-to-end.

You will also support budgeting, forecasting, and financial analysis - providing insights that inform decision‑making and help senior management drive performance and growth.

Your Responsibilities

Coordinate and support financial planning, budgeting, and forecasting cycles for divisions and portfolios, ensuring alignment with Development and Group strategy.
Maintain and interpret development project budgets and forecasts, partnering with Commercial Finance to review assumptions, ensure accounting outcomes, and report at both project and portfolio levels using Anaplan and Excel-based models.
Challenge and coordinate forecast inputs across specialist functions to improve accuracy and alignment.
Lead sub-workstreams within planning, budgeting, and forecasting processes, ensuring high-quality inputs.
Develop a strong grasp of commercial and accounting aspects across the portfolio.
Demonstrate expertise in three-way financial statements, financial ratios, and the interdependencies of key metrics.
Apply advanced analytics to identify trends, risks, opportunities, and variances, delivering actionable performance insights and recommendations.
Prepare clear, actionable reporting and insights for mid-level stakeholders, ensuring effective communication and well-structured PowerPoint presentations.
Build effective cross-functional relationships, including partnering with the Commercial Finance and Financial Control, while providing direct support to the Senior Finance Manager, Development FP&A.
Drive process improvements and efficiencies across financial planning and reporting activities

Your Point of Difference

To be successful, you will bring:

6-7 years' experience in chartered accounting and commercial environments (desirable)
CPA/CA qualification (essential)
Experience with business tools/systems such as Microsoft BI, Tableau, Salesforce, and Anaplan
Property industry exposure (desirable)
Strong stakeholder engagement and problem‑solving skills are essential
Strong written and verbal communication skills
Well‑developed analytical and problem‑solving skills
Strong technical accounting and financial modelling capability
Advanced Excel skills
Excellent attention to detail
Demonstrated ability to lead, motivate, and mentor finance team members
Passion, a self‑motivated can‑do attitude, and the ability to work to deadlines

Working at Mirvac

At Mirvac, our people are at the heart of everything we do. We foster a workplace built on trust, collaboration, inclusivity, and continuous learning. You'll join a supportive leader and an experienced team, with exposure across Commercial, Residential, and cross‑functional groups.

If you're an ambitious accounting professional ready to take the next step into FP&A within one of Australia's most respected property groups, we'd love to hear from you.

Finance Analyst
 
 

Finance Analyst role supporting a high-impact Claims division within a leading insurer
Key role delivering financial and operational insights across a $2B claims portfolio
Join a collaborative, high-performing finance team on a 12-month contract
 
An exciting opportunity has become available for a Finance Analyst to support the Claims Finance team within a leading insurance organisation. This 12-month contract will provide additional support to the team while a senior team member is on leave.

This role plays an important part in supporting the finance business partners across the largest and most complex division of the organisation, helping to analyse operational and financial data, support reporting, and provide insights that assist with decision making across the claims portfolio.

My client is seeking a motivated and analytical finance professional who enjoys working with large datasets, partnering with stakeholders, and delivering meaningful insights within a fast-paced environment.

Who will you be working with?

You’ll be joining a well-established insurance organisation known for delivering strong products and services that support customers in their time of need.
You’ll work closely with the Claims Finance team and report into a senior finance leader, while supporting finance business partners across the division.

What are the benefits?

Opportunity to work within a high-performing and experienced finance team
Exposure to large-scale financial and operational data across a $2B claims portfolio
Work on meaningful analysis that supports business performance and decision making
Supportive and collaborative team environment
Opportunity to further develop data analytics and finance systems experience
 
What will you be doing?

Support Finance Business Partners with financial and operational analysis across the Claims division
Deliver monthly management reporting, operational reporting, and analysis as required
Analyse daily, weekly, and monthly performance data to identify trends and insights
Support budgeting, forecasting, and reforecasting processes across the division
Assist with ad-hoc financial analysis and reporting requests
Extract and analyse data from finance systems and data sources to support reporting
Help consolidate and interpret complex financial and operational datasets
Identify opportunities to improve reporting processes and data accuracy
Translate financial results and data insights into clear information for operational stakeholders
Collaborate with Corporate Finance, Data & Insights teams, and wider stakeholders
 
What do you need?

Relevant tertiary qualification in Finance, Accounting, or a related discipline
CA or CPA qualification (or working towards) highly regarded
Minimum 3 years’ experience in a Finance Analyst or Financial Analysis role
Strong analytical capability and experience working with complex data
SQL knowledge highly beneficial for working with large datasets
Experience with TM1 or similar financial reporting systems advantageous
Strong stakeholder engagement and communication skills

Make a tangible impact in Agricultural Insurance Strategy. Lead end-to-end pricing activities with high autonomy.

About the Organisation

Achmea Australia is dedicated to keeping farmers farming. As a specialist agricultural insurer, we work closely with the farming community to protect their livelihoods. Sitting within the Agricultural Strategy function, this role is pivotal in ensuring our pricing remains competitive, sustainable, and fair. You will be joining a team where your technical expertise directly influences decision-making at the executive level.

About the Role

We are seeking an experienced Pricing Specialist to take ownership of end-to-end pricing activities. Reporting to the Senior Pricing Manager, you will operate with a high degree of autonomy, blending deep technical analysis with commercial strategy.

This is not just a back-room modelling role. You will be a key voice in the business, translating complex data into clear insights for stakeholders ranging from Underwriting and Claims to Executive leadership. You will also have the unique opportunity to liaise with the global Achmea international pricing community, with potential opportunities for international travel.

Key Responsibilities

You will manage regular pricing reviews and drive technical excellence across our portfolio. Your core focus areas will include:

End-to-End Pricing Management: overseeing portfolio profitability, incorporating technical modelling learnings, and accounting for claims inflation and regulatory changes.

Technical Modelling: Developing, maintaining, and refining technical models across relevant lines of business, ensuring alignment with Group protocols.

Commercial Impact: Analysing and applying commercial adjustments to optimise conversion and retention rates.

Strategic Insight: Monitoring the competitive landscape and external factors (such as regulatory changes like the Cyclone Reinsurance Pool, and macroeconomic trends) to inform pricing strategy.

Stakeholder Engagement: Presenting high-quality reporting and analysis to internal stakeholders and the Executive team.

Data & Innovation: Creating data sets to support pricing initiatives, monitor outcomes, and assisting in the design of new pricing algorithms.

About You

You are a decisive and result-oriented professional who can see the bigger picture behind the data. You possess the ability to work autonomously while maintaining strong relationships across the business.

To be successful, you will need:

Experience: Minimum of 5 years in General Insurance pricing (BAU or transformation). Experience with Domestic Home, Fire, ISR, Commercial Motor, or Liability classes is highly preferred.

Qualifications: A tertiary qualification in Actuarial Studies, Mathematics, Statistics, Data Science, or a similar quantitative field.

Technical Stack: Strong proficiency in Excel and general insurance modelling tools (e.g., Emblem, Radar, Earnix). Strong experience using R and/or Python is essential.

Analytical Mindset: Excellent problem-solving skills with the ability to spot trends, understand data limitations, and devise strategies to improve performance.

Communication Skills: The ability to articulate complex analytical results in a professional, easy-to-understand manner to non-technical stakeholders.

Why Join Us?

Competitive salary

Five weeks annual leave + additional volunteer leave 

ANZIIF qualifications and RG146 compliance (or a commitment to obtaining them)

Life insurance and salary continuance cover 

Study support and professional development opportunities. 

A career with purpose, autonomy, and the chance to make a real difference. 

If you are ready to combine your passion for rural communities with a rewarding career, we would love to hear from you. Together, we can help secure the future of Australian farming.

Company description:



Deloitte Services Pty Ltd



Job description:




ITS EDW Data Engineer | Data & Analytics | Sydney, AUS

Work on cutting-edge enterprise data solutions using Databricks, Azure Data Factory, DBT, and Python.

Collaborate with a high-performing, agile team to deliver impactful insights across Deloitte.

Continuous professional growth through mentoring, training, and exposure to emerging data engineering technologies.

Deloitte Technology is seeking a skilled Data Engineer to join our Enterprise Data Warehouse (EDW) team in Sydney. This is a permanent role where you'll play a key part in designing, implementing, and optimising scalable data pipelines and workflows that power Deloitte's data-driven initiatives. You'll have the opportunity to work closely with EDW pod leads and the Senior Data Engineering Manager, contributing to high-impact projects across the business.

What will your typical day look like?

Day to day you will:

Develop and maintain scalable data pipelines using Databricks and Azure Data Factory.

Transform raw data into actionable insights using Data Build Tools (DBT) and Python.

Build and maintain data integrations across systems using APIs.

Monitor and optimise workflows for performance and cost-efficiency.

Lead project delivery pods, mentor junior team members, and conduct code reviews.

Collaborate with data modelers, analysts, DBAs, DevOps, and infrastructure teams to deliver high-quality solutions.

Document processes, workflows, and system architecture to maintain clarity and knowledge sharing.

About the team

Deloitte Technology's EDW team powers enterprise-wide data initiatives, enabling business users to make informed, data-driven decisions. You'll join a collaborative and high-performing team working in an agile environment, tackling complex data challenges and continuously improving the EDW platform.

Enough about us, let's talk about you

You may have all or some of the following skills / experiences:

5+ years of experience in data engineering, with expertise in Databricks, SQL, Python, Azure Data Factory, and DBT.

Experience designing, building, and optimising data pipelines and workflows.

Hands-on experience with API-based data integrations and version control systems (Azure DevOps, GitHub).

Strong understanding of data architecture patterns and cloud data storage solutions.

Excellent problem-solving skills, attention to detail, and adaptability.

Collaborative mindset with strong communication and stakeholder management skills.

#LI-Hybrid

Wisr is committed to building a supportive, inclusive and diverse workplace, and we strongly encourage applications from all backgrounds and identities. We’re happy to accommodate any reasonable adjustments to the interview process to ensure equal opportunity for all. If you require reasonable adjustments, please reach out to us via careers@wisr.com.au.  

Our Why, What and How 

We are a proudly purpose-led ASX-listed fintech on a mission to bring people closer to financial wellness and make a real difference in the world, starting right here in Australia. By building products and experiences designed to have a positive impact on the financial health and lives of our customers, we are inching ever-closer to achieving our purpose. 

We offer smarter, fairer loans that help people kick their goals sooner. Wisr App, awarded Winner of WeMoney's 2024 & 2025 'Best Mobile Experience', makes loan self-service easy and offers free financial wellness tools including credit scores, round ups, money hacks and more..

The better we do this, the more positive change and impact we can have on our customers. Now is the time to join one of Australia’s fastest growing fintechs and make an impact.

We are a people first business and value flexibility as part of our work - our team works in a hybrid working environment, 3 days per week in our beautiful office space. 

About the Role

Wisr is scaling its core lending business while building new products that deepen customer value and improve unit economics. We are looking for a Head of Product who brings deep lending expertise, strong execution discipline, technical fluency, and strategic clarity.

This role exists to set product direction, strengthen governance and operating rhythm, and elevate product craft across squads, ensuring we consistently ship the highest-impact work.

You combine deep expertise in lending, product, and technology with commercial judgment and leadership maturity.

What you’ll do

The role is anchored around five core capability areas as detailed below -

Execution Excellence — Constant delivery outcomes

• You ensure disciplined, predictable portfolio execution without becoming a squad-level backlog owner.

Partner with Engineering, Design, Data, Operations, Growth and Risk  leaders to ensure squads have clear goals, priorities and executable plans.

• Guide trade-offs across squads and manage dependencies to maintain a reliable delivery cadence.

• Strengthen product governance: decision logs, outcome tracking, instrumentation and post-launch learning loops.

• Embed experimentation discipline (smoke tests, prototypes, controlled releases) with clear success metrics and decision points.

• Create transparency across the product portfolio so leadership understands progress, risk and return.

Lending Expertise — Depth of craft

• You understand the Australian lending landscape in depth and have a point of view on what creates differentiation.

• Deep understanding of the Australian lending market, competitors and structural trends.

• Strong understanding of pricing mechanics and lending economics — comfortable modelling business cases

• Actively engage in credit policy and risk meetings to improve product attractiveness without compromising credit integrity.

• Deep understanding of regulatory obligations in Australia, including responsible lending, DDO and CDR.

Immersed in underwriting and collections performance, using insights to improve product and portfolio outcomes.

Technology & Data Mindset — Technical fluency

• You are a technically and data-native product manager.

• Work closely with Engineering to define scalable, pragmatic technical approaches that deliver business outcomes at speed.

• Champion data-led decision-making across squads; thrive in product analytics and instrumentation.

• Ensure product decisions are grounded in measurable customer behaviour and portfolio performance.

• Stay close to emerging technologies (AI, Distributed Ledgers) that may unlock structural advantage.

• Translate complex technical constraints into clear commercial trade-offs.

Strategic Mindset — Set direction

• You provide clarity on what Wisr builds, why it matters, and how it creates durable advantage.

• Define and evolve a clear product vision and roadmap aligned to Wisr’s company strategy and innovation agenda.

• Articulate the future of Wisr’s lending and adjacent products — features, pricing, economics and competitive differentiation.

• Lead annual and quarterly product planning, translating strategy into roadmaps, OKRs and investment cases.

• Make explicit trade-offs across the portfolio to maximise long-term value creation.

• Partner with Risk, Finance and Legal to ensure product strategy aligns with regulatory and funding constraints.

People and Product Leadership — Raising the bar

• You build capability, not dependency.

• Lead, mentor and develop Product Managers, fostering customer obsession, learning and psychological safety.

• Set clear standards for product craft: problem framing, PRDs, experimentation, metrics and stakeholder management.

• Introduce pragmatic best practices that improve outcomes — not process for process’ sake.

• Foster strong cross-functional collaboration and shared accountability with Design, Engineering, Data and Delivery.

• Represent Product in executive forums with clarity, credibility and commercial depth.

What you’ll bring

• 10+ years of product management experience, with 5+ years leading Product Managers in fintech, SaaS or high-growth technology environments.

• Demonstrated success launching and scaling products (0→1 and 1→n).

• Strong understanding of lending economics, credit risk, technology platforms and regulated environments.

• Proven ability to define product vision and align cross-functional teams to measurable outcomes.

• Data-driven mindset with experience in analytics and experimentation.

• Systems thinker who moves comfortably between strategy and delivery detail.

• Exceptional communicator able to influence senior stakeholders.

• Experience with AI-enabled products (preferred but not essential).

What success looks like in your first 12 months:

• Cohesive product vision — A clearly articulated two-year roadmap aligned to business goals and strategic OKRs.

• Predictable execution — Stable priorities, visible governance, and consistent delivery against agreed outcomes.

• Stronger lending performance — Improvements in conversion, STP rate, credit performance and unit economics.

• Validated new products — Successful launch and validation of new initiatives meeting defined PMF criteria.

• Stronger team capability — High engagement and measurable lift in product craft.

• Data-led culture — Portfolio decisions grounded in experimentation and measurable outcomes.

Some of Our Perks & Benefits

• Flexible and hybrid working

• $500 every year to spend on your wellbeing

• Take an extra Annual Leave day off on us every financial year, with A Day on Wisr

• Access via WHEREFIT to discounted gym memberships, corporate discounts for wellbeing products and more!

• Generous paid parental leave to support your transition to parenthood

• Unlimited Udemy access to learn new skills

• Regular social events and awesome team offsites

• Access to our Employee Assistance Program, Uprise with up to 6 coaching sessions per year

Working at Wisr

As a purpose-led organisation on a mission to bring people closer to financial wellness, we truly believe in what we do and every person who joins our team plays a part in our success. By practising what we preach (hello, $500 wellness reimbursement, 1:1 coaching via Uprise and unlimited Udemy learning!), you are empowered to own your role, your development and the space that you play in.

We value psychological wellbeing and safety, fostering a high-performing culture which enables continuous learning and growth. Our hiring and interviewing process is designed for you to showcase your experience, skills and passion and take great pride in not just ‘ticking a box’. If this role resonates with you, we want to hear from you. 

Wisr Values

• Courageously ask, “What if?”: We challenge the status quo, and we know that decisions that make us smarter, stronger, faster, more innovative always start with a “What if?”.

• ❤️ for the customer: Our internal and external customers are at the center of everything we do. We genuinely have their best interests at heart.

• Smarter parts together: We believe the real magic happens when we bring together the right mix of Smart Parts and work as a team. 

• Be a stunning colleague: It’s not just about excelling in our roles, but also going the extra mile to make our colleagues and Wisr shine too. 

• Growth over comfort: We love learning, and we always find opportunities to learn something new whether it is facing a fear or pushing through something uncomfortable to level up. 

We are dedicated to creating an amazing candidate journey for every applicant. There's no 'shouting into the void' here. We're committed to responding to every single applicant.

About the Company
A well-established organisation within the property sector is expanding its Group Finance team. The business provides specialist advice that supports investment and commercial decision-making across Australia. This growth reflects the organisation’s continued expansion and a number of strategic initiatives currently underway.

About the Role
This newly created Finance Analyst position offers broad exposure across the business. You will work on strategic projects, M&A activity, and financial planning and analysis, providing insights and support to senior stakeholders. This is a dynamic role designed to give a variety of experience and the opportunity to influence key business decisions.

Key Duties

Develop and maintain financial models to support investment decisions and strategic initiatives
Conduct scenario and sensitivity analysis to assess commercial risks and opportunities
Support strategic projects with ad-hoc modelling and analysis
Interpret complex financial information and prepare clear recommendations
Present findings to senior stakeholders and executive leadership
About You
You are commercially minded, analytical, and curious, with a strong technical grounding in accounting and a clear understanding of P&L, balance sheet, and cash flow. You will have 5–7 years’ experience, starting in accounting and progressing into a commercially focused role, most likely at the Commercial Analyst or FP&A Analyst level. Experience in commercial finance, business analysis, or financial analysis roles
Exposure to M&A lifecycle activities highly regarded
CA/CPA qualified

Pay.com.au is a high-growth fintech redefining B2B payments, helping over 50,000 businesses process billions in payments and earn billions in points through its PayRewards program. They turn everyday business spend into extraordinary value through transfer partners such as Qantas, Virgin Australia and Singapore Airlines.

Founded in Melbourne, the company sits at the intersection of payments, credit and loyalty. Businesses use the platform to pay suppliers, manage expenses and earn rewards.

The company has grown rapidly in recent years, with revenue more than doubling in FY2025. As part of its next stage of growth, Pay.com.au is expanding internationally and preparing for a potential ASX listing.

The Role

We're looking for an AI Adoption Lead to help identify and implement practical ways AI can improve how the business operates.

This is not a product management role, nor is it a pure engineering position. Instead, the focus is on experimentation, rapid prototyping and applying AI to real operational problems across the organisation.

The goal is to find someone who enjoys building and testing things. Someone who is constantly experimenting with new AI tools, thinking about practical use cases, and building lightweight solutions that make teams more effective.

A large part of the role will involve identifying friction in existing workflows, testing whether AI can improve them, building simple MVPs and iterating based on real feedback from teams.

The emphasis is on speed, experimentation and practical outcomes rather than large transformation programs.

What You’ll Be Doing

Identify opportunities where AI can improve internal workflows across operations, marketing, sales and other teams
Rapidly prototype AI-powered tools, automations or workflows
Test ideas quickly and iterate based on feedback from internal teams
Work with engineers and data teams to safely deploy useful solutions
Experiment with emerging AI tools and frameworks
Help build internal momentum and capability around AI adoption
Translate AI capabilities into practical business applications
This role reports directly to a senior leader in the business and will work closely with engineering, data, marketing and operations teams.
Areas Where AI Could Make an Impact

Customer verification and operational workflows that currently involve manual steps
Marketing asset creation and campaign experimentation
Extracting insights from sales conversations and internal communications
Agent-based workflow automation across internal systems
These areas are starting points rather than a fixed roadmap. The expectation is that the person hired will identify additional opportunities as they go.

Technology Environment

The role will interact with a range of internal systems and data sources, including AWS infrastructure, Databricks and other operational platforms used across the business.

You’ll also work closely with engineers, analysts and data scientists who can help support the development and deployment of useful solutions.

What Success Looks Like

In the first three to six months, success will come from building working prototypes and demonstrating clear value for internal teams. Examples might include automating a repetitive operational task, building a tool to extract insights from internal data, or creating an AI workflow to speed up a marketing or sales process.

Over time, the goal is to embed AI into several core workflows across the business and help teams work more efficiently.

About You

This role will suit someone who spends a lot of time experimenting with AI tools and thinking about how to apply them to real problems.

You don’t need to be a deep machine learning specialist, but you should be comfortable working with modern AI tooling.

You must be someone who:

Regularly experiments with new AI tools and models
Enjoy building prototypes, MVPs and small automation tools
Think about how AI can improve real workflows
Are comfortable working independently in a lean environment
Are confident in challenging existing processes
Have experience working with tools such as Python, LLM APIs, automation platforms or modern AI frameworks
Supporting Information

Hybrid role, 3 days in and 2 at home. Office currently in Elsternwick, soon to relocate to Windsor
Salary: $120-150k + super, negotiable for someone exceptional
Interested?

Apply now or email matt@ponyexpresstalent.com

Ready to step into a role where your data expertise directly shapes workforce strategy? This is your chance to own high-impact analytical work while enjoying exceptional flexibility, an incredible leadership team, and a culture that genuinely values its people.

Client Details

Our client is a major, nationally recognised organisation undergoing an exciting period of transformation. They are known for their fantastic culture, genuine commitment to employee well being, and high-performing, supportive leadership team. With a strong track record of internal mobility and ongoing development, this is an environment where you can grow your career while enjoying industry‑leading work‑from‑home flexibility.

Description

As the Senior Workforce Data Analyst, you will play a critical role in shaping workforce efficiency and labour optimisation across the business. You will partner directly with senior Finance, Commercial, Payroll and operational stakeholders, using large-scale datasets, advanced modelling, and AI-driven insights to support strategic decisions.

Key focus areas include:

Translating complex labour and workforce data into actionable insights for non‑technical leaders
Designing and deploying AI and machine‑learning models to understand labour cost variations, productivity drivers, and optimisation opportunities
Building executive-ready dashboards and briefings (Power BI) highlighting trends, risks, and improvement outcomes

Profile

Amazing WFH and workplace flexibility- designed to support work‑life balance

Job Title: Snr Functional & Technical Analyst D365 SCO
Ad Text:
About us:
Snackbrands Australia is part of Intersnack Group, a private German snack food company founded in 1968. Although internationally owned we are still very much a local company Creating Happy Snacking Moments for everyone!


Here at Snack Brands Australia, we proudly make some of Australia’s favourite snacks including Kettle, CC’s, Thins, Cheezels, The Natural Chip Company and Nice & Natural. Across our three sites, we make over 200 million packets of chips a year - that's a lot of enjoyment and fun we bring to snackers every day! We encourage a culture that is exciting, challenging and we strive for innovation in everything we do. Our aim is for our people to share in our success, enjoy our products and reap the benefits of their hard work within a supportive environment where work life balance is encouraged!


About the role:


We’re looking for an experienced Snr Functional & Technical Analyst D365 SCO to join our IT Applications team and drive the successful delivery of D365 projects, enhancements and support across our Operations function.

This role will be based in Orchard Hills with hybrid working 

What you’ll be responsible for:


Leading the transition from legacy systems to Dynamics 365 F&O Supply Chain modules, performing fit-gap analyses and configuring modules to meet specific business workflows..
Conduct workshops with stakeholders to define business requirements.
Develop critical project artifacts, including Business Requirement Specifications (BRS), Functional Design Documents (FDD), technical requirements and process flows.
Reduce the Production and Logistic reliance on legacy solutions by migrating to Dynamics 365 Operations functionality.
Conduct Dynamics F&O working sessions to demonstrate system capabilities by configuring core modules, data migration and reporting to enable workflows that meet business requirements.
Partner with Finance, Production, and Logistics teams to ensure seamless and optimised end-to-end data flow and reporting.
Support supply chain and manufacturing users including production problems, incidents and change requests i.e. manage and resolve open tickets in your support queue.
Follow ITIL processes and methodologies for approval and migration of changes to production
Collaborate with Operations, Project Office and IT teams to resolve issues across operations, manufacturing, warehousing, distribution and inventory.
Partner with supply chain and operations departments across the business to ensure solutions meet operational requirements and align with business milestones.
Coordinate project plans, budgets and reporting.
Influence best practice solution architecture and support a high-performance IT culture.
Work with vendors to provide support and upgrade information.
Develop, execute and lead unit, system and user testing as required to implement changes into production
Build strong relationships across stakeholders and business users. 

What you’ll bring to the role:
Proven experience as a Senior Functional and Technical analyst in Dynamics F&O Supply Chain modules.
Experience in FMCG processes including order management, demand and supply planning, MRP, manufacturing, warehouse management, inventory management, procurement.
Excellent Dynamics 365 and business process knowledge in Supply Chain modules.
Solution knowledge of Dynamics 365, SQL data base and integration technologies.
Excellent communication, stakeholder engagement and influencing skills.
Strong analytical, project planning and commercial acumen.
Ability to manage multiple initiatives, navigate conflicting priorities and work with senior leaders 


What can you expect in return?
A supportive working environment (with plenty of snacks on hand!) where you'll feel engaged and valued, career growth, Birthday Leave, Access to discounts on Retailers, onsite parking (including electric car charging stations) and a focus on health and wellness (like Wellbeing Days!) to make your workplace more enjoyable!



**Our application process includes an Application Form that will be emailed to you directly after your submission. Please ensure you check your junk inbox for this application form, as only applications with this form will be considered. Thank you

The Role

At Trideca, we are looking for a bold, hands-on Chief Technology Officer who is excited by the opportunity to build something truly significant  — your own AI practice from the ground up.


Opportunities like this are rare!!! Your core mandate will be to build and scale Trideca's AI capability, with the autonomy and support to develop, grow, and commercialize AI solutions that deliver meaningful outcomes for both Trideca and our enterprise clients. 

As Trideca's most senior technology leader, this role offers the chance to define the vision, shape the capability, and lead the next phase of growth for both the traditional engineering team and AI-driven transformation. You will play a pivotal role in establishing how AI becomes embedded in our services, our solutions, and the value we deliver to some of Australia's largest organizations.

We are looking for a leader who loves getting into the detail with engineers, architecting world-class solutions, and leading by example. This is a true lead-by-doing role where you will work alongside brilliant technologists, challenge thinking, and ensure everything we deliver is technically exceptional and commercially impactful.

You will be responsible for creating and scaling Trideca's AI capability in addition to the engineering roadmap, where you will define the strategy, build and develop the team, develop the offerings, and take cutting-edge technology solutions to enterprise clients. From shaping our AI roadmap to winning new opportunities and delivering transformative engineering programs, you will sit at the center of how Trideca grows its technology footprint.

We are looking for a practical visionary, someone who sees the possibilities of AI but also knows how to turn ideas into real outcomes, not just another pilot. You're energized by emerging technologies, passionate about best practice, and excited by the challenge of building something meaningful from day one.

You have partnered with enterprise clients, led complex technology initiatives, and know how to connect technology, strategy, and commercial value. You're curious, ambitious, and thrive in environments where you can challenge the norm and push boundaries.

If you have ever wanted the autonomy and backing to build and scale an AI practice inside a respected technology consultancy, this is that opportunity.

How will you make an impact:

Lead and shape Trideca's next phase of growth by setting the strategic direction for how technology and AI are leveraged internally and for our clients.

Establish and scale a greenfield AI practice, defining the vision, capability roadmap, service offerings, and commercial strategy to position Trideca as a leading AI transformation partner.

Build and grow a high-performing team of engineers, consultants, and strategists who are passionate about solving complex problems and delivering exceptional outcomes for clients.

Develop strong executive relationships with key clients, deeply understanding their strategic objectives and aligning Trideca's technology and AI solutions to deliver measurable value.

Drive go-to-market initiatives that connect emerging technologies, AI capabilities, and customer outcomes to generate new business opportunities and expand Trideca's market presence.

Partner closely with the executive leadership team to turn strategic ideas into actionable outcomes, identifying new growth opportunities across technology, AI, and digital transformation.

Design and deliver pragmatic transformation roadmaps that leverage data, automation, and artificial intelligence to solve real business challenges and deliver lasting commercial impact.

Foster a culture of experimentation, excellence, and accountability, encouraging teams to innovate, challenge the status quo, and deliver high-quality solutions at pace. As a leader, you will champion best practice and continuously evolve our processes and delivery methodologies.

Own the financial performance of the technology function, including budget management, forecasting, cost centre accountability, and ensuring sustainable growth and profitability.


About You
﻿You are a builder and strategic leader with a passion for impact. You've built technology practices before, and you know what it takes to blend vision, innovation, and flawless execution.

To be successful in this role, you will bring:

Demonstrated experience designing, building, and scaling AI, machine learning, and data-driven solutions within complex technology environments.

Strong understanding of modern AI development frameworks and data platforms with the ability to translate emerging AI capabilities into practical, enterprise-ready solutions.

Hands-on credibility across the full AI lifecycle, from data strategy and model development to deployment, governance, and commercial application.

Proven success building and leading a technology practice from the ground up - from winning and growing new customers, expanding capability, and building teams to managing the P&L. We want to understand the successes, failures, and learnings from your experience.

Deep experience in consulting or professional services, ideally within a complex enterprise environment.

Having a customer-first delivery focus is imperative for this role. Trideca is not a product environment, and alignment to our company value of Customer Comes First is paramount.

Strong commercial acumen. You understand how decisions impact profitability, growth, and client value within your department.

A strong personal network and ability to identify and generate new business opportunities that align with Trideca's strategy the drive for growth both individually and as a business is critical, we all want to win at Trideca!

Exceptional leadership skills. This role doesn't come with an abundance of people at your disposal, we have a small but powerful internal team, and everyone is hands-on and accountable.

Clear thought leadership on AI adoption and business integration You are the expert, the advisor and the go-to person for our future tech landscape at Trideca.

A hands-on, builder mindset - you will be comfortable moving between vision and execution and bringing the team with you.

Have served in a leadership role for more than 5 years.

Have led teams of at least 30 people and scaled teams by 40% per year.
﻿

Professional traits that are necessary for this leader:

Exhibits excellent judgment

Hires great people. Develops great people.

Has relentlessly high standards

Thinks strategically, but stays on top of tactical execution

Thinks big and stands firm in their convictions

Results-oriented

Has the innate ability to inspire passion in others


Join us and shape the next era of digital transformation: www.trideca.com.au

About Us

Praemium is a leading provider of next generation wealth management solutions, empowering advisers to deliver exceptional outcomes for high net worth clients.

The recent acquisition of Technotia Laboratories brings world class scientific, engineering and design expertise in house, significantly strengthening Praemium’s advanced technology, computing and machine intelligence capabilities.

By combining trusted wealth management experience with proprietary innovation, Praemium is setting a new benchmark for automation, precision and scalable advice delivery.

Continuous Improvement. Intelligent innovation. Client-first outcomes. The Praemium way.

The Role

We are seeking an experienced Senior Investment Analyst to join our Investment Managers & Governance team. This is a senior, high-impact role responsible for helping ensure Praemium’s Investment Menus remain relevant and compliant in an increasingly complex regulatory environment.

Reporting to the Head of Investment Governance, you will play a critical role in driving fund review, oversight, and investment governance across the platform. You will conduct in-depth due diligence on external investment managers across a broad range of asset classes, provide ongoing investment monitoring, and present recommendations to Investment Committees to support informed decision-making. This role is ideally in Sydney, with a hybrid working model (approximately 2–3 days per week in the office)

This role is suited to a seasoned investment professional looking to step into a senior governance-focused position with meaningful influence over platform investment outcomes.

Key Responsibilities

Conduct comprehensive investment and organisational due diligence on external fund managers across multiple asset classes, including single-strategy and multi-manager investment
Produce high-quality research, analysis, and written due diligence reports to support investment recommendations for Platform Investment Menu
Present investment analysis, monitoring outcomes, and recommendations to Investment Committees and other senior stakeholders
Undertake ongoing investment monitoring, including periodic manager reviews, performance and returns analysis, and identification of investments requiring further investigation
Prepare and coordinate attribution analysis in liaison with fund managers
Manage and maintain investment manager data, research databases, and analytical tools
Support onboarding of new investment managers and related operational activities

Desired Qualifications, Experience and Competencies

Degree in Finance or a related discipline
CFA qualification or progress toward CFA accreditation is highly regarded
Minimum 7 years’ experience in multi-manager fund research, asset consulting, and/or fund ratings (essential)
Proven experience researching funds across multiple asset classes
Strong understanding of investment markets, platform financial products, and their use by financial advisers and clients
Experience with managed fund structures and Model / SMA operations (desirable)
Familiarity with retail investment and superannuation regulation, including trustee requirements (highly regarded)
Experience presenting investment recommendations and reviews to Investment Committees and managing complex stakeholder expectations
Outstanding written, verbal, and presentation communication skills
Demonstrated ability to exercise sound investment judgement and influence outcomes through well-reasoned recommendations
Advanced capability in Microsoft Excel; experience with Morningstar Direct and FactSet preferred

What We Offer

Permanent full-time opportunity
Flexible hybrid work environment, up to 2 days from home
Annual bonus opportunity
Access to novated leasing, financial wellness programs and learning opportunities
Tailored professional development
Modern office with end-of-trip facilities at 264 George Street, Sydney

How to Apply

Please apply with your CV, emphasising how your experience and career aligns with the key responsibilities this role. Applicants must have full, unrestricted Australian working rights. Police checks apply to successful candidates.

Job Title: Senior Systems Engineer, UDS Data Management
Ad Text:
Senior Systems Engineer, Data Management

Our field sales professionals rely on proactive technical support during the sales process – and our expert Systems Engineering team always steps up to the mark. We lead the development and implementation of complex and specialized products, applications, services and solutions. From delivering sales presentations and product demonstrations, to developing detailed installation or system integration plans, we ensure customers get the innovative, relevant, interoperable solutions they need.

Join us to do the best work of your career and make a profound social impact as a Senior Systems Engineer on our Systems Engineering Team in Chatswood.

What you’ll achieve

In this role, you will operate at the intersection of technology, customer success, and business impact. You will design modern data and AI architectures, translate complex requirements into clear use cases, and shape compelling solutions that drive our customers’ data and AI transformation.

You will:

Build trusted relationships with customers and lead discovery to understand their goals, challenges, and technical needs.

Design and validate modern cloud architectures for data, analytics, and AI/GenAI/RAG solutions.

Create clear solution designs, proposals, and technical materials that guide customers through their Data & AI transformation.

Deliver impactful demos and PoCs that highlight value, feasibility, and business outcomes.

Communicate complex data and AI concepts in a simple, customer-friendly way to both technical and non-technical stakeholders.

Evaluate modern data and AI platforms and explain how our solutions stand out in the competitive landscape.

Take the first step towards your dream career

Every Dell Technologies team member brings something unique to the table. Here’s what we are looking for with this role:

Essential Requirements

Technical Skills

Hands-on experience with leading cloud & data platforms
(Snowflake, Databricks, BigQuery, Redshift, Synapse, Cloudera) plus at least one major cloud (AWS, Azure or GCP).

Strong data architecture & engineering foundation
Data warehousing, data lakes/lakehouse, SQL, ETL/ELT, data modeling (star/snowflake), performance tuning, governance, and compliance (GDPR/CCPA).

Experience with data pipelines & unstructured data
Integration, orchestration (Airflow, dbt, Spark, Kafka), batch/streaming, and processing of PDFs, logs, images, text into structured/vectorized formats.

Familiarity with AI/GenAI & RAG concepts
Vector databases (pgvector, Elasticsearch, Milvus), embeddings, document processing, semantic search, chunking, and RAG pipeline fundamentals.

Broad analytics & BI knowledge
BI tools (Power BI, Tableau, Looker, Qlik), self-service analytics enablement, and end‑to‑end solution design from raw data to dashboards and models.

Consulting Skills

Strong customer-facing communication & storytelling
Ability to explain complex topics clearly to technical/non‑technical audiences; delivering presentations to senior stakeholders (CIO/CDO).

Discovery, requirements analysis & POC excellence
Skilled at uncovering business drivers, structuring PoCs, defining success criteria, and demonstrating solutions that showcase customer value.

Proven Experience

5+ years in a customer‑facing technical role such as Sales Engineer, Solutions Architect, Data Engineer, Analytics Consultant, or Data Scientist with strong commercial exposure.

Track record delivering modern Data & AI solutions
Experience in cloud DWH/lakehouse migrations, BI modernization, GenAI/RAG implementations, streaming analytics, or advanced analytics programs.

Desirable Requirements

Hands-on experience with ML/AI workflows & tools
Feature engineering, model development, notebooks, Python, Spark, MLflow, TensorFlow/PyTorch, LangChain, LlamaIndex.

Ability to translate ambiguous business problems
Into clear data, analytics, or AI use cases with measurable value.

Understanding of competitive Data & AI landscapes
Ability to articulate differentiation across platforms (data warehouses, lakehouses, analytics tools, AI ecosystems).

Who we are

We believe that each of us has the power to make an impact. That’s why we put our team members at the center of everything we do. If you’re looking for an opportunity to grow your career with some of the best minds and most advanced tech in the industry, we’re looking for you.

Dell Technologies is a unique family of businesses that helps individuals and organizations transform how they work, live and play. Join us to build a future that works for everyone because Progress Takes All of Us.
